articles.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 9 Including Lebanese Army Officers After Ceasefire Deal
articles.content:
贝鲁特（美联社）——黎巴嫩军方和国家媒体表示，以色列周六对黎巴嫩南部的空袭造成9人死亡，其中包括3名黎巴嫩军人。几天前，双方刚刚达成新的停火协议。

黎巴嫩总统约瑟夫·奥恩称此次针对其军事力量的袭击是“对国家主权和国际法的公然侵犯”，正值“持续升级威胁南部稳定与安全”之际。

黎巴嫩军方表示，对连接纳巴蒂耶市与马尔贾云镇道路上一辆汽车的空袭造成一名准将、一名上尉和另一名士兵死亡，但未公布他们的姓名。国有国家通讯社称，另一次对萨克萨基耶村的空袭造成6人死亡、4人受伤。

黎巴嫩军方表示，“持续、蓄意和反复的以色列侵略”旨在阻挠寻求解决方案的努力，“这些努力本可恢复稳定、达成全面停火并促使以色列从被占领的黎巴嫩领土撤军”。

以色列军方证实袭击了一辆汽车，称该车在收到“具体情报”后“可疑地”驶向卡夫·提卜尼特村附近的士兵，该情报显示伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党武装将从同一地区向以色列士兵开火。

以色列军方表示，他们针对的是真主党，而非黎巴嫩军队。

与此同时，以色列军方宣布了两名士兵在黎南部阵亡，但未公布死亡日期。

最新停火协议在华盛顿宣布，是通过美国斡旋的以色列与黎巴嫩政府之间的谈判达成的。黎巴嫩政府指责真主党将国家拖入战争，并在最新冲突前努力解除其武装。真主党拒绝接受该停火协议。

以色列军队已占领黎巴嫩约五分之一的领土，深入该国南部的程度超过1982年至2000年占领期结束以来的任何时候。自3月2日真主党向以色列北部发射火箭弹以来，已有超过3500人在黎巴嫩丧生。两天前，以色列和美国开始袭击伊朗。

以色列此后发动了对黎巴嫩的地面入侵，并实施了导致超过100万人流离失所的攻击。战斗已造成至少31名以色列士兵和3名平民死亡。

周五，奥恩和黎巴嫩总理批评伊朗反对最新停火协议，称德黑兰不应将他们国家作为与华盛顿谈判的“讨价还价筹码”。伊朗希望与美国的停火协议能涵盖黎巴嫩的局势。

伊朗外交部长阿巴斯·阿拉格希周六在X平台上回应称，在奥恩发表上述言论后，“人们会以为占领黎巴嫩五分之一领土、驱逐四分之一黎巴嫩人并每天轰炸其国家的是伊朗”。

“如果黎巴嫩真是伊朗的筹码，我们早就达成协议了。总统先生，从您真正的敌人手中拯救黎巴嫩吧，”阿拉格希补充道，他指的是以色列。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BEIRUT (AP) — Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed nine people including three members of the Lebanese military, the Lebanese army and state media said, days after the two sides reached a new ceasefire deal.

Lebanon’s President Joseph Aoun called the strike against its military a “flagrant violation” of the country’s sovereignty and international law during an “ongoing escalation that threatens stability and security in the south.”

Advertisement

An airstrike on a vehicle on a road linking Nabatiyeh city with Marjayoun town killed a brigadier general, a captain and another soldier, the army said, without releasing their names. Another airstrike on Saksakiyah village killed six people and wounded four, the state-run National News Agency said.

Lebanon’s army said the “continued, deliberate, and repeated Israeli aggression” aims to thwart efforts toward a solution “that would restore stability, establish a comprehensive ceasefire and lead to the Israeli withdrawal from the occupied Lebanese territories.”

The Israeli military confirmed hitting a vehicle and said it was “moving suspiciously” toward soldiers near Kfar Tibnit village, after the military received “concrete indications” that the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group would direct fire toward Israeli soldiers from the same area.

Advertisement

The military said it operates against Hezbollah and not against the Lebanese army.

Meanwhile, Israel’s military announced the deaths of two soldiers in southern Lebanon, without the dates they died.

The latest ceasefire, announced in Washington, came through U.S.-brokered talks between Israel and Lebanon’s government, which accuses Hezbollah of dragging the country into war and made efforts to disarm it before the latest hostilities. Hezbollah has refused the truce.

Advertisement

Israeli troops have seized around a fifth of Lebanon, pushing farther into the country’s south than at any time since the end of Israel’s 1982-2000 occupation. More than 3,500 people have been killed in Lebanon since the war began March 2 when Hezbollah fired rockets at northern Israel, two days after Israel and the U.S. began attacking Iran.

Israel has since launched a ground invasion of Lebanon and carried out attacks that have displaced more than 1 million people. The fighting has killed at least 31 Israeli soldiers and three civilians.

On Friday, Aoun and Lebanon’s prime minister criticized Iran for opposing the latest ceasefire deal, saying Tehran should not use their country as a “bargaining chip” in its talks with Washington. Iran wants a ceasefire deal with the United States to include the situation in Lebanon.

Advertisement

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi responded Saturday on X, saying that after Aoun’s comments, “one would think it’s Iran that has occupied a fifth of Lebanon, displaced a quarter of Lebanese and is bombing his country on daily basis.”

“Had Lebanon been a bargaining chip for Iran, we’d have a deal long ago. Save Lebanon from your real foe, Mr. President,” Araghchi added, in reference to Israel.

Related
IsraelIran warLebanonHezbollah

articles.title: Israeli Troops Kill 7-Month-Old Baby In West Bank, Palestinian Officials Say
articles.content:
约旦河西岸希伯伦（美联社）——巴勒斯坦卫生部称，以色列军队在占领的约旦河西岸向一辆汽车开枪，击中一名7个月大的巴勒斯坦婴儿面部，导致其死亡，并伤及其父母。

以色列军方表示，士兵们在希伯伦附近向一辆被认为正在加速驶向他们的车辆开枪。初步调查显示，三名受伤的巴勒斯坦人是无关平民。

自2023年10月加沙战争开始以来，以色列军事活动以及定居者对巴勒斯坦人的暴力事件在该地区激增。

婴儿的父亲、伯利恒大学讲师法赫德·阿布·海卡尔告诉美联社，周五晚上，一颗子弹击中挡风玻璃后穿透他的右手，击中坐在后座的儿子和妻子。据目击汽车的美联社记者报道，另一颗子弹击中引擎盖。

子弹穿过了萨姆·法赫德·阿布·海卡尔的面部。

“他是我的整个世界，”海卡尔在谈到这个周五刚满七个月大的男孩时说。

母亲伤势危重，弹片靠近心脏。

婴儿的遗体被包裹在巴勒斯坦国旗中。他的父亲抱着他。人们将这个小包裹放在脚下，鞠躬祈祷。

父亲要求正义。“最后他们会告诉你这是一次错误，”他说。“没有什么被称为错误。”

婴儿的祖母费里亚尔·阿布·海卡尔也在车上。她说，他们在一个检查站附近行驶，看到远处的以色列军车和士兵后停了下来。她说她最初以为枪声是警告射击。

“看到7个月大的婴儿面部碎裂的场景是可怕的，”她说。“世界上什么样的军队会这样做？”

**在这种情况下，以色列士兵很少受到惩罚**

据以色列人权组织“耶什·丁”称，2016年至2024年间，共有2427起关于不当行为的投诉，但被起诉的案件不足1%。

周六，巴勒斯坦红新月会表示，8人在定居者对纳布卢斯附近胡瓦拉镇的袭击中受伤，包括催泪瓦斯吸入和橡皮涂层金属子弹造成的伤害。

以色列军方表示，在据报发生牲畜被盗后，以色列人和巴勒斯坦人之间爆发了骚乱，双方使用石块和棍棒，士兵驱散了人群。军方表示，已知悉显示士兵对巴勒斯坦人使用暴力的视频，正在审查中。画面似乎显示有人被摔倒在地并遭到殴打。

胡瓦拉近年来遭受了多次袭击。2023年2月，在一名巴勒斯坦枪手杀害两名定居者后，大量以色列定居者在该地横冲直撞，烧毁了数十辆汽车和房屋。

联合国上月表示，自2023年10月7日哈马斯领导的武装分子袭击以色列南部并引发加沙战争以来，约旦河西岸和东耶路撒冷已有超过1000名巴勒斯坦人被杀，其中包括至少240名儿童。

超过70万以色列人生活在被占领的约旦河西岸和东耶路撒冷，这些地区是1967年以色列从约旦手中夺取的，巴勒斯坦人寻求在此建立未来的国家。国际社会压倒性地认为以色列在这些地区的定居点建设是非法的，是和平的障碍。

**加沙空袭至少造成7人死亡**

据沙法医院称，以色列周六在加沙城的空袭造成至少7人死亡，包括两名妇女、一名女孩和她的父亲，均来自同一家庭。院长穆罕默德·阿布·塞利米亚说，里马尔学校附近的一个帐篷被击中。

以色列军方表示，他们袭击了该地区所谓的武装分子。

与此同时，哈马斯表示，由哈利勒·哈亚赫主持的谈判代表在开罗开始了新一轮会谈，埃及、卡塔尔和土耳其的调解人参与，旨在打破僵局，推进实施10月生效的加沙停火协议。上一轮会谈是一个月前举行的。

发言人哈齐姆·卡西姆说，会谈旨在全面实施停火第一阶段，并结束以色列在该地区几乎每天发动的袭击。不稳定的停火协议于10月开始。虽然最激烈的战斗已经减弱，但停火协议期间几乎每天都有以色列开火。

监督停火协议的美国创建的和平委员会主席尼古拉·姆拉德诺夫上月承认，由于解除哈马斯武装这一关键问题，停火协议陷入僵局。

卡西姆说，会谈将讨论“各方可接受的方式”来实施第二阶段，包括处理巴勒斯坦激进组织的武器问题以及国际稳定力量的进入。

——萨米·马格迪开罗报道，萨姆·梅迪克特拉维夫报道
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

HEBRON, West Bank (AP) — Israeli troops shot at a car in the occupied West Bank, killing a 7-month-old Palestinian baby and wounding his parents, the Palestinian health ministry said, with the bullet striking the boy in the face.

The Israeli military said that soldiers shot at a vehicle perceived to be accelerating toward them near Hebron. It said an initial inquiry found that the three Palestinians wounded were uninvolved civilians.

Advertisement

Israeli military activity, and settler violence against Palestinians, have surged in the territory since the war in Gaza began in October 2023.

The baby’s father, Bethlehem University lecturer Fahd Abu Haikal, told The Associated Press that a bullet struck the windshield before piercing his right hand and striking his son and wife in the back seat Friday evening. Another bullet struck the hood, according to AP journalists who saw the car.

The bullet passed through Sam Fahd Abu Haikal’s face.

“He was the entire world,” Haikal said of the boy, who turned seven months on Friday.

The mother was in critical condition, with shrapnel close to her heart.

The baby’s body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag. His father carried him. The men placed the small bundle at their feet and bowed in prayer.

Advertisement

The father demanded justice. “At the end they tell you it was a mistake,” he said. “Nothing is called a mistake.”

The baby’s grandmother, Feryal Abu Heikal, was also in the car. She said that they were driving near a checkpoint and stopped when they saw Israeli military vehicles and soldiers in the distance. She said she initially thought the gunfire was warning shots.

“The scene was horrific to see a 7-month-old baby with a smashed face,” she said. “What kind of army in the world does this?”

Advertisement

Israeli Soldiers Are Rarely Punished In Such Cases

Israeli soldiers accused of harming Palestinians are seldom penalized, and were indicted in fewer than 1% of cases based on 2,427 complaints alleging wrongdoing between 2016 and 2024, according to Israeli rights group Yesh Din.

On Saturday, the Palestinian Red Crescent said that eight people were wounded in settler attacks on the town of Huwara, near Nablus, including from tear gas inhalation and rubber-coated metal bullets.

Advertisement

Israel’s military said that riots broke out between Israelis and Palestinians, with rocks and batons, after a reported theft of livestock and soldiers dispersed them. The military said that it was aware of footage showing a soldier using violence against a Palestinian and it was under review. Images appeared to show someone being thrown to the ground and beaten.

Huwara has seen numerous attacks in recent years. In February 2023, scores of Israeli settlers rampaged there, burning dozens of cars and homes, after a Palestinian gunman killed two settlers.

The United Nations said last month that more than 1,000 Palestinians including at least 240 children have been killed in the West Bank and east Jerusalem since the war in Gaza began with the attack by Hamas-led militants on southern Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.

Advertisement

More than 700,000 Israelis live in the occupied West Bank and east Jerusalem, territories captured by Israel in 1967 from Jordan and sought by the Palestinians for a future state. The international community overwhelmingly considers Israeli settlement construction in these areas to be illegal and an obstacle to peace.

Strike Kills At Least 7 In Gaza

An Israeli strike on Saturday in Gaza City killed at least seven people, including two women, a girl and her father, all from the same family, according to Shifa Hospital. Director Mohamed Abu Selmiya said that a tent near the Rimal school was struck.

Israel’s military said that it attacked what it called militants in the area.

Meanwhile, Hamas said that negotiators chaired by Khalil al-Hayyah began a new round of talks in Cairo with mediators from Egypt, Qatar and Turkey, in an effort to break a deadlock and move forward in implementing the Gaza ceasefire deal that took effect in October. The previous round of talks was a month ago.

Advertisement

Spokesperson Hazem Qassem said the talks were aimed at fully implementing the ceasefire’s first phase and ending Israel’s near-daily attacks in the territory. The fragile ceasefire began in October. While the heaviest fighting has subsided, the truce has seen almost daily Israeli fire.

Nickolay Mladenov, the head of the U.S.-created Board of Peace that oversees the ceasefire, acknowledged last month that the truce has stalled over the key issue of disarming Hamas.

Qassem said the talks will discuss “approaches acceptable to all parties” to implement the second phase, including addressing weapons of Palestinian militant groups and the entry of an international stabilization force.

Advertisement

___

Samy Magdy reported from Cairo, and Sam Mednick from Tel Aviv, Israel.

Related
IsraelPalestine

Adblock test (Why?)

articles.title: U.S. Military Says It Shot Down Iranian Missiles, Drones Launched Toward Gulf Allies, Strait Of Hormuz
articles.content:
布里奇沃特（美联社）——美国军方表示，伊朗于周五向霍尔木兹海峡和海湾阿拉伯盟友发射弹道导弹和无人机，美方予以拦截并对伊朗部分沿海监视雷达站进行打击。这次交火进一步破坏了与德黑兰之间本已脆弱的停火协议。

交火发生在特朗普政府加大力度施压伊朗达成协议以结束冲突之际。

美国中央司令部周五晚在社交媒体上表示，伊朗向科威特和巴林发射了七枚弹道导弹，美军拦截了六枚，第七枚未能击中目标。军方表示，没有收到美军人员伤亡的报告。

在这些弹道导弹发射之前，美国当天早些时候还击落了四架向霍尔木兹海峡方向飞行的伊朗无人机。

美国中央司令部在社交媒体上表示：“这些攻击无人机对地区海上交通构成直接威胁。”

科威特军方表示，部队正在拦截攻击该国的导弹和无人机。巴林则启动了空袭警报，指示居民转移到最近的安全地点并遵循官方指示。

据国家通讯社伊拉纳报道，伊朗革命卫队称其目标是科威特阿里萨勒姆空军基地（该基地驻有美军）以及美国海军第五舰队在海湾小国巴林的驻地。

美军正在对伊朗港口实施封锁，以回应德黑兰对这一全球石油和天然气运输关键通道的控制。此举导致能源价格飙升，并在11月中期选举前给特朗普总统的共和党带来政治压力。

美国中央司令部表示，击中雷达站（包括海峡中的一个岛屿）“是为了防御进一步袭击”。

**特朗普承诺迅速结束美伊冲突**

这是双方你来我往攻击的最新一轮，这些攻击使战争中的脆弱停火以及达成延长停火协议的努力更加紧张。本周早些时候，伊朗无人机严重损坏了科威特主要机场的客运航站楼，造成一人死亡、数十人受伤，机场短暂关闭。

尽管这些攻击引发了对停火可能崩溃的新担忧，但特朗普周五对记者表示，“伊朗局势似乎进展得相当顺利”。

“我们将非常迅速地退出伊朗，无论是通过一份文件还是通过非常强硬的手段，结果都会非常有力，”特朗普在威斯康星州与农民举行的活动中表示。“非常强硬的手段可能反而更容易，但我们会走出来，你们的化肥价格会大幅下降，就像四个月前那样。”

特朗普似乎越来越被困在一场陷入僵局的冲突中。美国和伊朗谈判代表一周前达成了初步协议，同意将停火延长60天，并就伊朗核计划启动新一轮谈判。但特朗普要求进行未指明的修改，而伊朗官员没有表现出签署协议的公开迹象。

当被问及周五为何需要这么长时间时，特朗普告诉NBC《会见新闻界》节目说，这是因为“这对他们来说非常困难”，并提到他们的“极大独立性”以及“他们强大、他们骄傲”的事实。

“有些他们从未想过要做的事情，他们将不得不做。他们别无选择，这需要一点时间，”他在采访中说。

特朗普表示，伊朗人仍有21%到22%的导弹。

**以色列对黎巴嫩空袭继续**

他的政府还宣扬了黎巴嫩政府和以色列本周在华盛顿经美国斡旋的谈判后达成的最新停火协议。然而，伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党武装组织拒绝接受该协议，新的攻击使其进一步面临风险。

以色列军方周五对黎巴嫩南部多个地区发动空袭，并向九个村庄发布疏散警告，其中包括一个收容了数千名因战斗而流离失所者的村庄。据国家通讯社报道，空袭在黎巴嫩南部六个地点造成九人死亡。

以色列军方表示，两名士兵在周五与黎巴嫩南部武装分子的交火中受伤，其中一人伤势严重。

黎巴嫩的战斗——以色列军队已占领了南部大片地区——也威胁到结束伊朗战争和重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的努力，因为伊朗要求任何持久停火都必须涵盖黎巴嫩。

除在霍尔木兹海峡拦截无人机外，美国军方表示，美军周五早些时候还在印度洋登上了一艘与伊朗有关联的受制裁油轮，美国试图阻止伊朗从石油和其他商品中获利。

美国还针对伊朗能源行业实施新制裁，对一组个人、公司和油轮实施制裁。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BRIDGEWATER, N.J. (AP) — The U.S. military said it shot down Iranian ballistic missiles and drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf Arab allies on Friday, while striking some of the Islamic Republic’s coastal surveillance radar sites in response, an exchange of fire that further frayed a shaky ceasefire with Tehran.

The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.

Advertisement

U.S. Central Command said on social media Friday night that Iran fired seven ballistic missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain, with U.S. forces intercepting six of the missiles and a seventh failing to reach its target. The military said there were no reports of harm to U.S. personnel.

The ballistic missiles were fired after the U.S. earlier in the day shot down four Iranian drones that were launched toward Strait of Hormuz.

“The attack drones posed an immediate threat to regional maritime traffic,” U.S. Central Command said on social media.

Advertisement

Kuwaiti’s military said forces were intercepting missiles and drones attacking the country, while Bahrain activated air raid sirens and told residents to move to the nearest safe location and follow official instructions.

Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it targeted the Ali Al Salem airbase, which hosts U.S. forces in Kuwait, and the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet in the tiny Gulf island nation of Bahrain, according to the state-run IRNA news agency.

The U.S. military is enforcing a blockade on Iranian ports in response to Tehran’s chokehold on the crucial corridor for global oil and natural gas shipments, which has sent energy prices spiking and posed political problems for President Donald Trump’s Republican Party ahead of the midterm congressional elections.

Advertisement

U.S. Central Command said it hit the radar sites, including an island in the strait, “to defend against further attacks.”

Trump Promises A Quick End To U.S.-Iran Conflict

It was the latest in back-and-forth attacks that have strained the tenuous ceasefire in the war and efforts to reach a deal to extend that truce. Earlier this week, Iranian drones heavily damaged a passenger terminal at Kuwait’s main airport, killing one person, wounding dozens and briefly closing the airfield.

Advertisement

Despite the attacks raising new concerns that the ceasefire could collapse, Trump told reporters Friday that “the situation with Iran seems to be going quite well.”

“We’re going to come out of Iran very quickly and it’s going to be very strong one way or the other, whether it’s a piece of paper or the very tough way,” Trump said at an event with farmers in Wisconsin. “The very tough way is maybe the easier way, but we’re going to come out, and your fertilizer prices are going to go way down, just like they were four months ago.”

Trump increasingly appears to be boxed in on a conflict that has settled into a holding pattern. U.S. and Iranian negotiators reached a tentative agreement a week ago to extend the ceasefire by 60 days and start a new round of talks on Iran’s nuclear program. But Trump has called for unspecified changes and Iranian officials have shown no public signs of signing off on the deal.

Advertisement

Asked on Friday why it was taking so long, Trump told NBC’s “Meet the Press” it was because “it’s a very hard thing for them,” citing their “great independence” and the fact that “they’re strong, they’re proud.”

“There are things they never thought they’d be doing that they’re going to have to do. They’ve got no choice, and it takes a little while,” he said in the interview.

Trump said the Iranians still have 21% to 22% of their missiles.

Israeli Strikes On Lebanon Continue

His administration also has touted the latest ceasefire agreed to this week by the Lebanese government and Israel after U.S.-brokered talks in Washington. However, the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group has rejected the agreement and new attacks have put it at further risk.

Advertisement

The Israeli military on Friday struck multiple parts of southern Lebanon and issued evacuation warnings for nine villages, including one that has sheltered thousands of people displaced by the fighting. The strikes killed nine people in six locations in southern Lebanon, the state news agency reported.

The Israeli military said two soldiers were wounded, one severely, in an encounter Friday with militants in southern Lebanon.

The fighting in Lebanon, where Israeli forces have seized large swaths of the south, also threatens efforts to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz because Iran has demanded that any lasting truce extend to Lebanon.

Advertisement

Besides the drone interception in the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said earlier Friday that its forces boarded a sanctioned oil tanker linked to Iran in the Indian Ocean as the United States seeks to prevent Iran from profiting off its oil and other goods.

The U.S. also targeted Iran’s energy sector with new sanctions on a group of people, firms and tankers.
